attachment.content:
- apex: Systematic implementation using APEX methodology (Analyze-Plan-Execute-Validate) with parallel agents and self-validation. Use when implementing features, fixing bugs, or making code changes that benefit from structured workflow.
- claude-memory: Create and optimize CLAUDE.md memory files or .claude/rules/ modular rules for Claude Code projects. Comprehensive guidance on file hierarchy, content structure, path-scoped rules, best practices, and anti-patterns. Use when working with CLAUDE.md files, .claude/rules directories, setting up new projects, or improving Claude Code's context awareness.
- codex-environment: Create, inspect, or repair Codex app Local Environment configuration for worktrees, including .codex/environments/environment.toml, setup and cleanup scripts, worktree up/down scripts, copied env files, isolated databases, and reusable app actions.
- commit: Quick commit and push with minimal, clean messages
- create-pr: Create and push PR with auto-generated title and description
- fix-errors: Fix all ESLint and TypeScript errors with parallel processing using snipper agents
- fix-grammar: Fix grammar and spelling errors in one or multiple files while preserving formatting
- fix-pr-comments: Fetch PR review comments and implement all requested changes
- merge: Intelligently merge branches with context-aware conflict resolution
- oneshot: Ultra-fast feature implementation using Explore → Code → Test workflow. Use when implementing focused features, single tasks, or when speed over completeness is priority.
- prompt-creator: Expert prompt engineering for creating effective prompts for Claude, GPT, and other LLMs. Use when writing system prompts, user prompts, few-shot examples, or optimizing existing prompts for better performance.
- ralph-loop: Setup the Ralph autonomous AI coding loop - ships features while you sleep
- skill-creator: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create a skill", "build a skill", "write a skill", "improve skill structure", "understand skill creation", or mentions SKILL.md files, skill development, progressive disclosure, XML structure, or bundled resources (scripts, references, assets). Comprehensive guide for creating effective Claude Code skills.
- subagent-creator: Expert guidance for creating, building, and using Claude Code subagents and the Task tool. Use when working with subagents, setting up agent configurations, understanding how agents work, or using the Task tool to launch specialized agents.
- ultrathink: Deep thinking mode - approach problems like a craftsman, obsess over details, and create elegant solutions
- workflow-apex-free: Systematic implementation using APEX methodology (Analyze-Plan-Execute-Validate) with parallel agents and self-validation. Use when implementing features, fixing bugs, or making code changes that benefit from structured workflow.
- fix-on-my-computer: Setup wizard - verify bun, install deps, run tests, fix until ALL pass
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

message.content:
question : c'est quoi ce problème ?
Ran 1 stop hook (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Stop hook error: Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh: ligne 1: afplay: commande introuvable

aiTitle: Fix afplay command not found error

content.text:
`afplay` est une commande macOS pour jouer des sons. Tu es sur Linux, donc elle n'existe pas.

Tu as probablement un **stop hook** dans tes settings Claude Code qui utilise `afplay` pour jouer un son quand Claude s'arrête. Il faut remplacer par l'équivalent Linux : `paplay` (PulseAudio) ou `aplay` (ALSA).

Tu veux que je regarde ta config pour corriger ça ?

attachment.stderr: Failed with non-blocking status code: /bin/sh: ligne 1: afplay: commande introuvable

message.content: modifie pour adapté a mon system (fedora linux 44 kde)

content.text: Je vais d'abord lire ta config Claude Code.
Fedora 44 utilise PipeWire — `paplay` fonctionne via la couche de compatibilité PulseAudio. Je remplace les deux occurrences.

aiTitle: Fix afplay command not found error

content.text:
Les deux hooks sont mis à jour (`afplay` → `paplay`). Si `paplay` n'est pas installé, tu peux l'ajouter avec :

```bash
sudo dnf install pulseaudio-utils
```

content: Fixed audio hooks in Claude Code settings, replacing macOS `afplay` with Linux `paplay` for both Stop and Notification hooks. No further action needed unless `paplay` is missing — install with `sudo dnf install pulseaudio-utils`. (disable recaps in /config)

message.content: existe t'il une version de claude desktop pour linux ?

input.prompt: Is there an official Claude desktop app for Linux? Check the Anthropic website and any recent announcements (2025). Report what platforms are supported and if Linux support is planned or available.

content.text:
## Summary

There is **no official Claude desktop app for Linux**. According to Anthropic's official download page, the Claude desktop app is explicitly marked as "Not available for Linux."

### Supported Platforms

**Official support:**
- **macOS**: 11 (Big Sur) or higher
- **Windows**: 10 or higher (x64 and arm64)

**Not supported:**
- Linux (explicitly unavailable)

### Linux Users' Options

While there's no official Linux support, Linux users have alternatives:

1. **Claude Code CLI** - Anthropic's official terminal-based tool that runs natively on Linux, macOS, and Windows. This is Anthropic's recommended solution for Linux users wanting an agentic coding assistant.

2. **Web version** - Access Claude through the browser at claude.com

3. **Community builds** - Unofficial community projects exist (e.g., `claude-desktop-debian` on GitHub), which repackage the application for Linux distributions, though these are not officially supported by Anthropic.

### Recent Updates (2025-2026)

There's no indication in recent announcements (through May 2026) that Linux desktop support is planned. Recent updates have focused on macOS and Windows features for the Claude Code desktop application, including parallel sessions, an integrated terminal, and improved diff viewing.

Sources:
- [Download Claude | Claude by Anthropic](https://claude.com/download)
- [Installing Claude Desktop | Claude Help Center](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/10065433-installing-claude-for-desktop)
- [Use Claude Code Desktop - Claude Code Docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop)
- [Anthropic Rebuilds Claude Code Desktop App Around Parallel Sessions](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/04/15/anthropic-rebuilds-claude-code-desktop-app/)
